docs.text: Ember on Occasion The year is 2024. You are 8 hours into Cyberpunk 2077, having waited until it went on sale. Life took you away for most of last year, but you've got 30 minutes on a Sunday night to hop back in. But you don't remember any of the buttons, and there's no way you're starting over to replay the tutorial. You give up and wash some dishes. If you don't work with Ember.js every day but enjoy leveraging it for the occasional project, you don't need the story-mode lessons of the Ember.js guides . This site is a work-in-progress designed to be a collection of quick references and patterns useful for the casual, infrequent developer using Ember.js. The focus will be on quick "how did I do that" usage of Ember.js, including the references required for import in case your editor of choice doesn't handle language servers perfectly. These docs are generally up-to-date for Ember.js 7.1 .
docs.title: Ember on Occasion

docs.text: The year is 2024. You are 8 hours into Cyberpunk 2077, having waited until it went on sale. Life took you away for most of last year, but you've got 30 minutes on a Sunday night to hop back in. But you don't remember any of the buttons, and there's no way you're starting over to replay the tutorial. You give up and wash some dishes. If you don't work with Ember.js every day but enjoy leveraging it for the occasional project, you don't need the story-mode lessons of the Ember.js guides . This site is a work-in-progress designed to be a collection of quick references and patterns useful for the casual, infrequent developer using Ember.js. The focus will be on quick "how did I do that" usage of Ember.js, including the references required for import in case your editor of choice doesn't handle language servers perfectly. These docs are generally up-to-date for Ember.js 7.1 .

docs.text: Templates Ember's templating language is a descendent of Handlebars . Ember templates are most often found in files with the extension .gjs or gts (depending on whether you are using TypeScript or not) inside of . This may be referred to as template tag or strict mode. There are other ways to use templates but we will not concern ourselves with those, for now. HTML Templates are written in a component with HTML within the
docs.title: Templates

docs.text: Ember's templating language is a descendent of Handlebars . Ember templates are most often found in files with the extension .gjs or gts (depending on whether you are using TypeScript or not) inside of <template></template> . This may be referred to as template tag or strict mode. There are other ways to use templates but we will not concern ourselves with those, for now.

docs.title: Comments

docs.text:
{{#if this.someThing}} Shown if someThing is true. {{else if this.anotherThing}} anotherThing is true. {{else if this.yetAnotherThing}} yetAnotherThing is true. {{else}} If nothing else. {{/if}}
This div adds the "is-active" class based on the argument @isActive.
docs.title: Conditionals

docs.text:
Iterate over a list:
    {{#each-in this.records as |record|}}
  • {{record}}
    • {{else}} Shown if this.records is empty, null, or undefined. {{/each}}
Iterate over a dictionary:
    {{#each-in this.dict as |key value|}}
  • {{key}} - {{value}}
    • {{/each}}
